items.title: Ukrainian drone strike on empty Baltic fuel depot prompts top-level resignation – in Latvia
items.snippet: A Ukrainian official said Russia is spoofing Ukraine's drones, diverting them to strike nearby Baltic nations instead of targets in Russia.

items.title: Ukraine ramps up ground robot production to spare soldiers, haul ammo — and rescue grandma
items.snippet: The same unmanned ground systems Ukraine uses to haul ammunition and evacuate wounded soldiers are now pulling civilians out of contested ground.

items.title: Hegseth aims to cut through the bureaucracy with ‘Deal Team Six’
items.snippet: The Pentagon is utilizing a crew of private sector businessmen to renovate how the department negotiates with defense companies.

items.title: US forces disable Iranian-flagged tankers trying to cross blockade
items.snippet: This week, U.S. forces around the Strait of Hormuz have launched strikes against Iranian military facilities and disabled missiles launched by the IRGC.

items.title: Turkey touts propellant breakthrough for Yıldırımhan long-range ballistic missile
items.snippet: The Turkish government has been working on hypersonic technologies and liquid-propellant propulsion systems for nearly a decade.

items.title: As the US Army adds drones to formations, here’s how one base trains its operators
items.snippet: Fort Stewart soldiers are figuring out how to train on unmanned systems after the base stood up the Marne Unmanned Center of Excellence.

items.title: US and Iran exchange fire as Trump says war will ‘be over quickly’
items.snippet: U.S. forces launched retaliatory strikes against Iranian military facilities Thursday after Iran fired missiles and drones at three U.S. Navy vessels.

items.title: Pentagon turns to AI targeting to help troops shoot drones
items.snippet: The technology can detect threats and distinguish them from non-threats, such as birds, faster than a human operator can.

items.title: France moves aircraft carrier to Red Sea with eye on Hormuz mission
items.snippet: France on Wednesday deployed its carrier strike group to the Red Sea as part of planning for a potential mission to secure the Strait of Hormuz.

items.title: B-52 engine replacement clears critical design review, first modifications to begin this year
items.snippet: The Air Force’s program to replace the B-52H Stratofortress’s 1960s-era engines cleared its critical design review, the service announced May 4.

items.title: Outgoing Leonardo boss touts ‘Michelangelo Dome,’ cyber tack as key achievements

items.title: France moves carrier into Red Sea ahead of potential Hormuz mission

items.title: Here’s what’s behind the US Army’s $2.1B R&D funding increase
items.snippet: Within the Army’s $253 billion budget request is a proposal to boost research and development spending by 12.9%.

items.title: Pentagon assures safe passage through Strait of Hormuz despite presence of mines
items.snippet: The guarantee follows a security warning from U.S. Naval Forces Central Command to vessels on Monday about the presence of mines in the strait.
